catalog.title: Define queue routing method intentionally
catalog.summary: Each queue should use a routing method chosen for the interaction model rather than inherited by accident.
catalog.why_it_matters: Routing method determines how arrival time, priority, and skills influence assignment.
catalog.recommended_state: Routing method is explicitly chosen and documented for each queue.
catalog.anti_pattern: Using a queue with default routing behavior that no one has reviewed.

catalog.title: Change queue scoring only when backlog is clear
catalog.summary: Set or change scoring during queue creation or while no interactions are waiting.
catalog.why_it_matters: Midstream scoring changes can produce unexpected assignment order for waiting interactions.
catalog.recommended_state: Scoring changes occur only during planned maintenance windows or at creation time.
catalog.anti_pattern: Changing scoring while interactions are already waiting in the queue.

catalog.title: Keep the skills taxonomy deliberate and current
catalog.summary: Skills should represent meaningful expertise and be assigned with discipline.
catalog.why_it_matters: Bloated or stale skill taxonomies reduce routing precision and raise maintenance cost.
catalog.recommended_state: Skills are narrow, meaningful, reviewed, and tied to real routing use cases.
catalog.anti_pattern: Accumulating dozens of overlapping or unused skills.

catalog.title: Use skill ratings only where they drive actual routing decisions
catalog.summary: Skill ratings should reflect real proficiency and should not be decorative.
catalog.why_it_matters: Ratings imply ordering logic; inaccurate ratings distort routing outcomes.
catalog.recommended_state: Ratings are periodically reviewed and used only where the routing method benefits from them.
catalog.anti_pattern: Uniform or arbitrary ratings that create false precision.

catalog.title: Align wrap-up codes to the queue division
catalog.summary: Wrap-up codes assigned to a queue should belong to the same division when possible.
catalog.why_it_matters: Division-aligned codes produce clearer reporting and reduce ambiguity across business units.
catalog.recommended_state: Queue wrap-up codes are governed by the owning division.
catalog.anti_pattern: Cross-division wrap-up catalogs without a reporting reason.

catalog.title: Keep the wrap-up catalog concise and semantically clear
catalog.summary: Wrap-up codes should describe outcomes distinctly, without synonym sprawl.
catalog.why_it_matters: Overlapping codes weaken reporting and make agent selection inconsistent.
catalog.recommended_state: Each wrap-up code has a unique purpose and plain-language meaning.
catalog.anti_pattern: Multiple codes that mean nearly the same thing.

catalog.title: Separate workitem queues by media type where practical
catalog.summary: Dedicated queues reduce routing contention between workitems and other interaction types.
catalog.why_it_matters: Mixed media can create priority side effects that starve other work.
catalog.recommended_state: Workitems and real-time media are isolated when operationally appropriate.
catalog.anti_pattern: Mixed-media queues that are difficult to tune.

catalog.title: Add agents to multiple queues cautiously
catalog.summary: Multi-queue membership should be deliberate and capacity-aware.
catalog.why_it_matters: Heavy multi-queue membership can affect backlog evaluation and routing behavior.
catalog.recommended_state: Membership is minimal, purposeful, and tied to staffing strategy.
catalog.anti_pattern: Broad 'add everyone everywhere' queue membership.

catalog.title: Implement explicit retry backoff for rate-limited requests
catalog.summary: Clients should handle rate limits using deliberate retry intervals and limit metadata inspection.
catalog.why_it_matters: This reduces repeated throttling and improves stability under load.
catalog.recommended_state: Integrations inspect limit information and back off rather than retrying aggressively.
catalog.anti_pattern: Immediate repeated retries after 429 responses.

catalog.title: Prefer notifications over polling for asynchronous state changes
catalog.summary: Use event subscriptions where available instead of repeated status polling.
catalog.why_it_matters: Notification-based patterns reduce request volume and improve responsiveness.
catalog.recommended_state: State-change monitoring is event-driven where the platform supports it.
catalog.anti_pattern: Tight-loop polling for campaign or resource state.

catalog.title: Use bulk endpoints for multi-record operations
catalog.summary: For large sets of records, bulk APIs should be preferred over repeated single-item requests.
catalog.why_it_matters: Bulk operations usually deliver better throughput and lower API overhead.
catalog.recommended_state: Bulk APIs are used whenever available for batch tasks.
catalog.anti_pattern: Loops of one-by-one mutations where a bulk endpoint exists.

catalog.title: Request the minimum OAuth scopes required
catalog.summary: Applications and automations should only request scopes they truly need.
catalog.why_it_matters: Least-privilege design reduces blast radius and eases governance review.
catalog.recommended_state: Each app has a documented scope set tied to a business purpose.
catalog.anti_pattern: Broad 'just in case' scope grants.

catalog.title: Design BYOC Premises Edge resiliency as N+1
catalog.summary: Capacity planning should tolerate the loss of one active Edge while preserving service continuity.
catalog.why_it_matters: A design that only works at full inventory is fragile.
catalog.recommended_state: Expected concurrent load remains supportable after single-Edge failure.
catalog.anti_pattern: Sizing that requires every Edge to stay healthy.

catalog.title: Ensure phones have primary and secondary Edge paths
catalog.summary: Phones should benefit from redundant registration paths rather than a single attachment point.
catalog.why_it_matters: Redundant assignment shortens service disruption during Edge failure.
catalog.recommended_state: Managed phones participate in primary/secondary redundancy design.
catalog.anti_pattern: Single-path phone dependencies in a supposedly redundant site.

catalog.title: Keep redundant Edge pools within compatible capacity classes
catalog.summary: Combine like-for-like or supported capacity classes within redundancy groups.
catalog.why_it_matters: Unsupported or uneven combinations can distort failover assumptions.
catalog.recommended_state: Redundant pools are composed of compatible Edge types and sizes.
catalog.anti_pattern: Mixing incompatible capacity classes without a supported design.

catalog.title: Maintain low latency and low jitter between dependent Edge components
catalog.summary: Network quality between Edges should stay within tight tolerances.
catalog.why_it_matters: Failover reliability and telephony quality both degrade under poor inter-Edge conditions.
catalog.recommended_state: Inter-Edge latency and jitter remain within recommended thresholds.
catalog.anti_pattern: Treating redundancy as valid while the underlying network is unstable.

catalog.title: Define primary and secondary sites explicitly for multi-site resiliency
catalog.summary: Multi-site configurations should declare the intended primary and secondary topology.
catalog.why_it_matters: Ambiguous multi-site design complicates failover expectations and operations.
catalog.recommended_state: Primary/secondary site relationships are explicit, documented, and tested.
catalog.anti_pattern: Multi-site deployments with no validated failover narrative.

catalog.title: Build flows for the organization, not by cloning examples blindly
catalog.summary: Examples should be references, not production designs copied without adaptation.
catalog.why_it_matters: Real routing, prompts, schedules, and failover logic are organization-specific.
catalog.recommended_state: Production flows are intentionally tailored and reviewed.
catalog.anti_pattern: Publishing example flows with minimal local design work.

catalog.title: Use descriptive and unique names for flows and prompts
catalog.summary: Names should be specific enough to support maintenance, reporting, and promotion.
catalog.why_it_matters: Weak names make troubleshooting and environment comparison harder.
catalog.recommended_state: Names encode business purpose and remain unique within the relevant type.
catalog.anti_pattern: Generic labels like Main, Test, Final2, or Copy.

catalog.title: Give Architect states distinct labels
catalog.summary: State labels should clearly describe the action or branch they represent.
catalog.why_it_matters: Architect readability and supportability depend on state clarity.
catalog.recommended_state: Sequence labels are explicit and non-duplicative.
catalog.anti_pattern: Reusing vague labels across many states.

catalog.title: Keep schedule groups timezone-explicit
catalog.summary: Schedule groups should make timezone behavior obvious and intentional.
catalog.why_it_matters: Hidden timezone assumptions create open/closed routing defects.
catalog.recommended_state: Timezone and holiday/emergency logic are documented and tested.
catalog.anti_pattern: Implicit timezone handling or copied schedule groups without review.

catalog.title: Use validation, versioning, and check-in/check-out discipline
catalog.summary: Flow changes should follow a controlled lifecycle before publication.
catalog.why_it_matters: This reduces accidental release errors and helps preserve change history.
catalog.recommended_state: Validation and version management are part of the deployment routine.
catalog.anti_pattern: Direct ad hoc publishing with no review or lifecycle control.

catalog.title: Default to least privilege
catalog.summary: Users, groups, and integrations should have only the permissions necessary for their purpose.
catalog.why_it_matters: Broad permissions increase blast radius and complicate governance.
catalog.recommended_state: Access is role-based, purposeful, and periodically reviewed.
catalog.anti_pattern: Blanket admin assignment for convenience.

catalog.title: Prefer custom roles over broad administrative grants
catalog.summary: Use narrower custom roles where they satisfy the need.
catalog.why_it_matters: This limits overexposure while preserving operational effectiveness.
catalog.recommended_state: Broad admin roles are rare and justified.
catalog.anti_pattern: Using Master Admin as a shortcut.

catalog.title: Keep the role catalog lean
catalog.summary: Avoid uncontrolled growth in role count and permission overlap.
catalog.why_it_matters: Role sprawl obscures intent and increases maintenance burden.
catalog.recommended_state: Roles are curated, named clearly, and periodically rationalized.
catalog.anti_pattern: Creating one-off roles for every exception.

catalog.title: Use divisions as explicit administrative boundaries
catalog.summary: Division design should reflect actual operational or business boundaries.
catalog.why_it_matters: Division scoping is a core containment and governance mechanism.
catalog.recommended_state: Division assignments are intentional and auditable.
catalog.anti_pattern: Cross-division access that exists only because it was easier.

catalog.title: Monitor telephony resource limits before warning thresholds become operational blockers
catalog.summary: Capacity should be reviewed before the environment approaches hard limits.
catalog.why_it_matters: Resource exhaustion often creates last-minute provisioning failures.
catalog.recommended_state: Limit utilization is tracked and actioned before it becomes critical.
catalog.anti_pattern: Discovering object-limit exhaustion during an urgent change.

catalog.title: Manage DID and extension assignment with clear ownership
catalog.summary: DIDs and extensions should be allocated from controlled ranges with documented stewardship.
catalog.why_it_matters: Ad hoc numbering plans create confusion and slow incident response.
catalog.recommended_state: Number assignment follows a maintained plan.
catalog.anti_pattern: One-off assignments with no ownership model.

catalog.title: Ensure users are properly addressable for group ring scenarios
catalog.summary: Group ring behavior depends on user telephony addressing being complete.
catalog.why_it_matters: Missing DID or extension coverage can lead to inconsistent alerting.
catalog.recommended_state: Group ring participants have the required telephony configuration.
catalog.anti_pattern: Assuming group ring will work without endpoint addressing prerequisites.

catalog.title: Prefer CX as Code for repeatable configuration management
catalog.summary: Supported Genesys Cloud configuration should be represented as managed text artifacts where practical.
catalog.why_it_matters: Text-defined configuration improves repeatability, review, and promotion between orgs.
catalog.recommended_state: Core configuration is source-controlled and deployable.
catalog.anti_pattern: Treating production configuration as manual-only tribal knowledge.

catalog.title: Promote changes through automated CI/CD pipelines
catalog.summary: Changes should advance through environments with validation and minimal manual handling.
catalog.why_it_matters: Automation reduces drift and improves consistency across orgs.
catalog.recommended_state: Dev, test, and production promotion is pipeline-driven.
catalog.anti_pattern: Manual copy/paste promotion across orgs.

catalog.title: Use managed Terraform state and locking
catalog.summary: Terraform-based Genesys workflows should use controlled remote state and locking mechanisms.
catalog.why_it_matters: Shared state without governance creates deployment risk.
catalog.recommended_state: State storage, locking, and promotion strategy are documented and enforced.
catalog.anti_pattern: Ad hoc local state files for shared environments.

catalog.title: Deploy through an automated, test-backed process
catalog.summary: Production changes should be the output of a repeatable deployment pipeline with validation steps.
catalog.why_it_matters: This improves consistency and supports change control.
catalog.recommended_state: Commit-driven deployment with validation gates.
catalog.anti_pattern: Direct human-driven production changes with no reproducible deployment path.
